title: Investigating Factors Influencing Internet Speed on UBC Vancouver Campus
excerpt:
Designed and ran a blocked factorial experiment measuring UBC's ubcsecure WiFi 
download speeds across campus building, building area (indoor/edge/outdoor), and 
time of day, treating speed test website as a blocking factor to isolate it from 
the effects of interest. ANOVA showed significant main effects of building, area, 
and time of day, plus significant interactions among all three, while device type, 
browser, and speed test website had no measurable effect, indicating the results 
reflect real network conditions rather than measurement artifacts.
